Explore documentation, sample code, tutorials, and more to help you build custom Webex apps.

Webex supports agentic architectures through Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and Agent-to-Agent (A2A) servers, enabling you to build intelligent, autonomous applications that can interact with Webex services.

2026 Cisco and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved.
By continuing to use our website, you acknowledge the use of cookies.
Privacy Statement
Change Settings
Consent Manager
Your opt out preference signal is honored.
Consent Manager
Your Privacy
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Performance Cookies
Targeting Cookies
Functional Cookies
Your Privacy
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. From the list on left, please choose whether this site may use Performance and/or Targeting Cookies. By selecting Strictly Necessary Cookies only, you are requesting Cisco not to sell or share your personal data. Note, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience on the site and the services we are able to offer.
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms.    You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Cookies Details
Performance Cookies
Performance Cookies
These cookies provide metrics related to the performance and usability of our site. They are primarily focused on gathering information about how you interact with our site, including: page load times, response times, error messages, and allowing a replay of a visitor’s interactions with our site, which enables us to review and analyze visitor behavior, helping to improve site usability and functionality. These cookies also allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site and will not be able to monitor its performance.
Cookies Details
Targeting Cookies
Targeting Cookies
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites.    They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.
Cookies Details
Functional Cookies
Functional Cookies
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Cookies Details

hits.title: uBlock Origin Maintainer on Chrome vs. Firefox WebExtensions
Tell HN: Cisco WebEx on OS X uses the same pre-installer tricks as Zoom
Show HN: Fed up with WebEx, so we built a way to screen share in under 20 sec.
Firefox 54: E10S-Multi, WebExtension APIs, CSS Clip-Path
Cisco: Magic WebEx URL Allows Arbitrary Remote Command Execution
Firefox 57 as the first release where only WebExtensions will be supported
Safari is adopting an extensions API similar to Firefox’s WebExtensions
Safari 14 added WebExtensions support, so where are the extensions?

analysis.name: Webex
analysis.value_proposition: Cisco's unified collaboration platform offering meetings, messaging, calling, contact center, and developer APIs for enterprise communication.
analysis.ideal_customer: Large enterprises and IT organizations needing integrated video conferencing, calling, messaging, and contact center with deep API extensibility and Cisco ecosystem integration.

features.name: video meetings
features.description: Integrated audio, video, and content sharing for secure web meetings
features.evidence_quote: leverage integrated audio, video, and content sharing with highly secure web meetings

features.name: messaging api
features.description: REST API for sending and receiving messages in spaces and 1:1 chat
features.evidence_quote: Get up and running in no time with a walk-through of the most popular Webex API: Messaging.

features.name: meetings api
features.description: Programmatic access to create, manage, and access meeting resources
features.evidence_quote: Webex Meetings APIs — Accessing Meeting Resources

features.name: calling api
features.description: APIs for Webex Calling workflows including PSTN and cloud calling
features.evidence_quote: Find out everything you need to know about Webex Calling workflows.

features.name: bots
features.description: Automated bots for messaging spaces, 1:1 chat, and external system notifications
features.evidence_quote: Bots can be added to messaging spaces or engage in 1:1 chat, or post notifications from external systems.

features.name: webhooks
features.description: Event-driven notifications to external apps when activities occur in Webex
features.evidence_quote: Use webhooks to notify your apps when specific activities occur in Webex.

features.name: embedded apps framework
features.description: Framework to embed third-party or custom apps directly inside the Webex client
features.evidence_quote: Access your app directly in Webex with the Embedded Apps Framework.

features.name: contact center apis
features.description: Extensible APIs to build, enhance, and customize customer experience solutions
features.evidence_quote: Webex Contact Center lets you build, enhance and customize CX solutions with a rich and extensible set of APIs.

features.name: contact center search api
features.description: Search API within the Webex Contact Center platform
features.evidence_quote: Get Started with the Search API — Contact Center

features.name: device control via xapi
features.description: Control Webex-registered room and desk devices programmatically using xAPI
features.evidence_quote: Learn how to control Webex devices using xAPI in a variety of scenarios.

features.name: workspace integrations
features.description: Integrations that connect Webex with physical workspace and device environments
features.evidence_quote: Devices — Workspace Integrations

features.name: administration api
features.description: APIs to manage, monitor, and audit Webex applications and users
features.evidence_quote: Manage, monitor and audit your Webex applications. Administration API Overview

features.name: compliance tools
features.description: Built-in compliance features for governance and regulatory requirements
features.evidence_quote: Webex Compliance

features.name: oauth integration
features.description: OAuth 2.0 authentication flow for third-party app integrations
features.evidence_quote: Run a Webex OAuth Integration Locally — Login with Webex

features.name: personal access token auth
features.description: Developer token-based authentication for API access
features.evidence_quote: Using a Personal Token

features.name: rest api
features.description: Full-featured REST API covering all major Webex platform capabilities
features.evidence_quote: Learn about the various features particular to the Webex REST API.

features.name: websocket support
features.description: Real-time event streaming via WebSockets through the JS SDK
features.evidence_quote: Using Websockets with the JS SDK

features.name: instant connect
features.description: One-click meeting links with no app install or login required for guests
features.evidence_quote: Use Instant Connect to make one-click calls, without the need for the Webex App or login credentials!

features.name: mcp server support
features.description: Model Context Protocol server support enabling agentic AI app integrations
features.evidence_quote: Webex supports agentic architectures through Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers

features.name: agent-to-agent (a2a) servers
features.description: A2A server support for building autonomous multi-agent AI architectures on Webex
features.evidence_quote: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) servers, enabling you to build intelligent, autonomous applications

features.name: agentic app framework
features.description: Platform-level support for building agentic, autonomous applications using Webex services
features.evidence_quote: Get Started with Agentic Apps — Webex MCP Server Guide

features.name: sdks and open source tooling
features.description: Official SDKs, widgets, tools, and community-supported open source projects
features.evidence_quote: Official SDKs, Widgets, and Tools — Sample Code and Applications — Community Supported Projects

features.name: bot starter kits
features.description: Open source starter kits to accelerate chatbot development on Webex
features.evidence_quote: Open Source Bot Starter Kits

features.name: app hub
features.description: Marketplace for discovering and publishing Webex-integrated third-party apps
features.evidence_quote: Webex App Hub — Submitting the Webex App Hub

features.name: screen sharing
features.description: In-meeting screen and content sharing capability
features.evidence_quote: Show HN: Fed up with WebEx, so we built a way to screen share in under 20 sec.

features.name: roomos device support
features.description: Developer integration with Cisco RoomOS-powered conference room devices
features.evidence_quote: RoomOS Articles — Device Developer's Guide

differentiators: MCP and A2A agentic architecture support for AI-native app development
Instant Connect — guest meeting access with no app or login required
Deep xAPI device control for Cisco room hardware
Cisco-backed enterprise compliance and administration APIs
Contact Center APIs with extensible CX customization

recent_ships.what: Agentic Apps support via MCP servers and Agent-to-Agent (A2A) servers
recent_ships.source: developer.webex.com docs — marked 'New': 'Webex supports agentic architectures through MCP servers and A2A servers'

recent_ships.what: Webex MCP Server and MCP Server Guide published for developers
recent_ships.source: developer.webex.com docs — 'Webex MCP Server Guide — Webex MCP Servers' listed under New

recent_ships.what: Contact Center section added/updated in developer docs

praised: Messaging API cited as the most popular Webex API (implied by docs prominence)
Large developer ecosystem with SDKs, open source tools, and learning labs
complaints: Screen sharing UX frustrating enough to motivate a competitor product — 'Fed up with WebEx, so we built a way to screen share in under 20 sec.'
Security vulnerability: 'Cisco: Magic WebEx URL Allows Arbitrary Remote Command Execution'
Aggressive installer behavior on macOS: 'Cisco WebEx on OS X uses the same pre-installer tricks as Zoom' — perceived as intrusive/untrustworthy

pricing.notes: Pricing page returned HTTP 403 Access Denied — no tier data available from provided sources.
